title: Fast O_{expected}(N) Algorithm for Finding Exact Maximum Distance in E^2 Instead of O(N^2) or O(N lg N)
title_zh: 用于查找 E^2 中精确最大距离的快速 O_{expected}(N) 算法，而不是 O(N^2) 或 O(N lg N)
abstract: This paper describes novel and fast, simple and robust algorithm with O(N) expected complexity which enables to decrease run-time needed to find an exact maximum distance of two points in E2. The proposed algorithm has been evaluated experimentally on larger different datasets. The proposed algorithm gives a significant speed-up to applications, when medium and large data sets are processed. It is over 10 000 times faster than the standard algorithm for 10^6 points randomly distributed points in E2. Experiments proved the advantages of the proposed algorithm over the standard algorithm and convex hull diameters approaches.
summary: This paper describes novel and fast, simple and robust algorithm with O(N) expected complexity which enables to decrease run-time needed to find an exact maximum distance of two points in E2. The proposed algorithm has been evaluated experimentally on larger different datasets. The proposed algorithm gives a significant speed-up to applications, when medium and large data sets are processed. It is over 10 000 times faster than the standard algorithm for 10^6 points randomly distributed points in E2. Experiments proved the advantages of the proposed algorithm over the standard algorithm and convex hull diameters approaches.
abstract_zh: 本文描述了新颖、快速、简单且鲁棒的算法，其预期复杂度为 O(N)，能够减少在 E2 中找到两点的精确最大距离所需的运行时间。所提出的算法已在更大的不同数据集上进行了实验评估。当处理中型和大型数据集时，所提出的算法可以显着加快应用程序的速度。对于 E2 中 10^6 点随机分布点，它比标准算法快 10 000 倍以上。实验证明了该算法相对于标准算法和凸包直径方法的优势。

AI.tldr: 提出一种在二维欧氏空间中寻找点集精确最大距离的快速算法，期望时间复杂度为O(N)
AI.motivation: 传统精确最大距离算法时间复杂度为O(N²)或O(N log N)，在处理中大型数据集时效率低下，亟需更快的算法来提升应用性能
AI.method: 设计一种新颖、简单且鲁棒的算法，通过期望线性时间复杂度的策略，避免全量点对比较或凸包直径计算
AI.result: 实验表明，对于100万个随机分布点，该算法比标准算法快10000倍以上，在多个不同数据集上均验证了其优势
AI.conclusion: 该算法显著优于标准算法和凸包直径方法，尤其适合中大型数据集的精确最大距离计算

title: Deep Patch Visual Odometry
title_zh: 深补丁视觉里程计
abstract: We propose Deep Patch Visual Odometry (DPVO), a new deep learning system for monocular Visual Odometry (VO). DPVO uses a novel recurrent network architecture designed for tracking image patches across time. Recent approaches to VO have significantly improved the state-of-the-art accuracy by using deep networks to predict dense flow between video frames. However, using dense flow incurs a large computational cost, making these previous methods impractical for many use cases. Despite this, it has been assumed that dense flow is important as it provides additional redundancy against incorrect matches. DPVO disproves this assumption, showing that it is possible to get the best accuracy and efficiency by exploiting the advantages of sparse patch-based matching over dense flow. DPVO introduces a novel recurrent update operator for patch based correspondence coupled with differentiable bundle adjustment. On Standard benchmarks, DPVO outperforms all prior work, including the learning-based state-of-the-art VO-system (DROID) using a third of the memory while running 3x faster on average. Code is available at https://github.com/princeton-vl/DPVO
summary: We propose Deep Patch Visual Odometry (DPVO), a new deep learning system for monocular Visual Odometry (VO). DPVO uses a novel recurrent network architecture designed for tracking image patches across time. Recent approaches to VO have significantly improved the state-of-the-art accuracy by using deep networks to predict dense flow between video frames. However, using dense flow incurs a large computational cost, making these previous methods impractical for many use cases. Despite this, it has been assumed that dense flow is important as it provides additional redundancy against incorrect matches. DPVO disproves this assumption, showing that it is possible to get the best accuracy and efficiency by exploiting the advantages of sparse patch-based matching over dense flow. DPVO introduces a novel recurrent update operator for patch based correspondence coupled with differentiable bundle adjustment. On Standard benchmarks, DPVO outperforms all prior work, including the learning-based state-of-the-art VO-system (DROID) using a third of the memory while running 3x faster on average. Code is available at https://github.com/princeton-vl/DPVO
abstract_zh: 我们提出了 Deep Patch Visual Odometry (DPVO)，这是一种用于单目视觉里程计 (VO) 的新深度学习系统。 DPVO 使用一种新颖的循环网络架构，旨在跨时间跟踪图像补丁。最近的 VO 方法通过使用深度网络来预测视频帧之间的密集流，显着提高了最先进的准确性。然而，使用密集流会产生大量的计算成本，使得这些先前的方法对于许多用例来说不切实际。尽管如此，人们仍然认为密集流很重要，因为它提供了针对错误匹配的额外冗余。 DPVO 反驳了这一假设，表明通过利用基于稀疏补丁的匹配相对于密集流的优势，可以获得最佳的准确性和效率。 DPVO 引入了一种新颖的循环更新算子，用于基于补丁的对应以及可微分束调整。在标准基准测试中，DPVO 的性能优于所有先前的工作，包括基于学习的最先进的 VO 系统 (DROID)，该系统使用三分之一的内存，同时运行速度平均提高了 3 倍。代码可在 https://github.com/princeton-vl/DPVO 获取

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于深度学习的单目视觉里程计系统DPVO，通过稀疏补丁匹配替代传统密集光流方法，在保证精度的同时显著提升计算效率。
AI.motivation: 现有视觉里程计方法依赖计算成本高昂的密集光流，而作者认为密集光流并非必要，通过稀疏补丁匹配可兼顾精度与效率。
AI.method: 设计递归网络架构跟踪图像补丁，结合稀疏补丁匹配与微分捆绑调整，替代传统密集光流计算。
AI.result: 在标准基准测试中，DPVO相较现有最佳方法（如DROID）内存消耗降低三分之二，平均速度提升3倍，同时保持最优精度。
AI.conclusion: DPVO证明了稀疏补丁匹配在视觉里程计中的有效性，为高效实时定位提供了新思路。

authors_text: Yujia Xu,Hak-Keung Lam,Guangyu Jia,Jian Jiang,Junkai Liao,Xinqi Bao
title: Improving COVID-19 CT Classification of CNNs by Learning Parameter-Efficient Representation
title_zh: 通过学习参数高效表示来改进 CNN 的 COVID-19 CT 分类
abstract: COVID-19 pandemic continues to spread rapidly over the world and causes a tremendous crisis in global human health and the economy. Its early detection and diagnosis are crucial for controlling the further spread. Many deep learning-based methods have been proposed to assist clinicians in automatic COVID-19 diagnosis based on computed tomography imaging. However, challenges still remain, including low data diversity in existing datasets, and unsatisfied detection resulting from insufficient accuracy and sensitivity of deep learning models. To enhance the data diversity, we design augmentation techniques of incremental levels and apply them to the largest open-access benchmark dataset, COVIDx CT-2A. Meanwhile, similarity regularization (SR) derived from contrastive learning is proposed in this study to enable CNNs to learn more parameter-efficient representations, thus improving the accuracy and sensitivity of CNNs. The results on seven commonly used CNNs demonstrate that CNN performance can be improved stably through applying the designed augmentation and SR techniques. In particular, DenseNet121 with SR achieves an average test accuracy of 99.44% in three trials for three-category classification, including normal, non-COVID-19 pneumonia, and COVID-19 pneumonia. And the achieved precision, sensitivity, and specificity for the COVID-19 pneumonia category are 98.40%, 99.59%, and 99.50%, respectively. These statistics suggest that our method has surpassed the existing state-of-the-art methods on the COVIDx CT-2A dataset.
summary: COVID-19 pandemic continues to spread rapidly over the world and causes a tremendous crisis in global human health and the economy. Its early detection and diagnosis are crucial for controlling the further spread. Many deep learning-based methods have been proposed to assist clinicians in automatic COVID-19 diagnosis based on computed tomography imaging. However, challenges still remain, including low data diversity in existing datasets, and unsatisfied detection resulting from insufficient accuracy and sensitivity of deep learning models. To enhance the data diversity, we design augmentation techniques of incremental levels and apply them to the largest open-access benchmark dataset, COVIDx CT-2A. Meanwhile, similarity regularization (SR) derived from contrastive learning is proposed in this study to enable CNNs to learn more parameter-efficient representations, thus improving the accuracy and sensitivity of CNNs. The results on seven commonly used CNNs demonstrate that CNN performance can be improved stably through applying the designed augmentation and SR techniques. In particular, DenseNet121 with SR achieves an average test accuracy of 99.44% in three trials for three-category classification, including normal, non-COVID-19 pneumonia, and COVID-19 pneumonia. And the achieved precision, sensitivity, and specificity for the COVID-19 pneumonia category are 98.40%, 99.59%, and 99.50%, respectively. These statistics suggest that our method has surpassed the existing state-of-the-art methods on the COVIDx CT-2A dataset.
abstract_zh: COVID-19大流行继续在世界范围内迅速蔓延，给全球人类健康和经济造成巨大危机。其早期检测和诊断对于控制进一步传播至关重要。人们提出了许多基于深度学习的方法来协助临床医生基于计算机断层扫描成像进行自动 COVID-19 诊断。然而，挑战仍然存在，包括现有数据集的数据多样性较低，以及深度学习模型的准确性和敏感性不足导致检测效果不佳。为了增强数据多样性，我们设计了增量级别的增强技术并将其应用于最大的开放访问基准数据集 COVIDx CT-2A。同时，本研究提出了源自对比学习的相似性正则化（SR），使CNN能够学习更多参数有效的表示，从而提高CNN的准确性和灵敏度。七个常用 CNN 的结果表明，通过应用设计的增强和 SR 技术可以稳定地提高 CNN 性能。特别是，具有 SR 的 DenseNet121 在三类分类（包括正常、非 COVID-19 肺炎和 COVID-19 肺炎）的三项试验中实现了 99.44% 的平均测试准确率。 COVID-19 肺炎类别的精确度、敏感性和特异性分别为 98.40%、99.59% 和 99.50%。这些统计数据表明，我们的方法已经超越了 COVIDx CT-2A 数据集上现有的最先进的方法。

AI.tldr: 本文提出通过学习参数高效的表示来改进CNN在COVID-19 CT图像分类中的性能。
AI.motivation: 现有深度学习方法在COVID-19 CT分类中面临数据多样性不足及模型准确率、敏感度不高的问题，需提升检测效果。
AI.method: 设计分层数据增强技术并应用于COVIDx CT-2A数据集，结合对比学习的相似性正则化（SR）优化CNN参数表示。
AI.result: 七种CNN在增强与SR技术下性能稳定提升，DenseNet121在三分类任务中测试准确率达99.44%，COVID-19类别精确率、敏感度、特异度分别为98.40%、99.59%、99.50%。
AI.conclusion: 所提方法在COVIDx CT-2A数据集上超越现有最佳方法，验证了增强技术与SR的有效性。

authors_text: Phong Nguyen-Ha,Lam Huynh,Esa Rahtu,Jiri Matas,Janne Heikkila
title: Cascaded and Generalizable Neural Radiance Fields for Fast View Synthesis
title_zh: 用于快速视图合成的级联和可概括的神经辐射场
comment: Accepted at IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence (TPAMI)
comments: Accepted at IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence (TPAMI)
abstract: We present CG-NeRF, a cascade and generalizable neural radiance fields method for view synthesis. Recent generalizing view synthesis methods can render high-quality novel views using a set of nearby input views. However, the rendering speed is still slow due to the nature of uniformly-point sampling of neural radiance fields. Existing scene-specific methods can train and render novel views efficiently but can not generalize to unseen data. Our approach addresses the problems of fast and generalizing view synthesis by proposing two novel modules: a coarse radiance fields predictor and a convolutional-based neural renderer. This architecture infers consistent scene geometry based on the implicit neural fields and renders new views efficiently using a single GPU. We first train CG-NeRF on multiple 3D scenes of the DTU dataset, and the network can produce high-quality and accurate novel views on unseen real and synthetic data using only photometric losses. Moreover, our method can leverage a denser set of reference images of a single scene to produce accurate novel views without relying on additional explicit representations and still maintains the high-speed rendering of the pre-trained model. Experimental results show that CG-NeRF outperforms state-of-the-art generalizable neural rendering methods on various synthetic and real datasets.
summary: We present CG-NeRF, a cascade and generalizable neural radiance fields method for view synthesis. Recent generalizing view synthesis methods can render high-quality novel views using a set of nearby input views. However, the rendering speed is still slow due to the nature of uniformly-point sampling of neural radiance fields. Existing scene-specific methods can train and render novel views efficiently but can not generalize to unseen data. Our approach addresses the problems of fast and generalizing view synthesis by proposing two novel modules: a coarse radiance fields predictor and a convolutional-based neural renderer. This architecture infers consistent scene geometry based on the implicit neural fields and renders new views efficiently using a single GPU. We first train CG-NeRF on multiple 3D scenes of the DTU dataset, and the network can produce high-quality and accurate novel views on unseen real and synthetic data using only photometric losses. Moreover, our method can leverage a denser set of reference images of a single scene to produce accurate novel views without relying on additional explicit representations and still maintains the high-speed rendering of the pre-trained model. Experimental results show that CG-NeRF outperforms state-of-the-art generalizable neural rendering methods on various synthetic and real datasets.
abstract_zh: 我们提出了 CG-NeRF，一种用于视图合成的级联和可推广的神经辐射场方法。最近的泛化视图合成方法可以使用一组附近的输入视图来呈现高质量的新颖视图。然而，由于神经辐射场均匀点采样的性质，渲染速度仍然很慢。现有的特定于场景的方法可以有效地训练和渲染新颖的视图，但不能泛化到未见过的数据。我们的方法通过提出两个新颖的模块来解决快速和泛化视图合成的问题：粗略辐射场预测器和基于卷积的神经渲染器。该架构基于隐式神经场推断出一致的场景几何形状，并使用单个 GPU 高效地渲染新视图。我们首先在 DTU 数据集的多个 3D 场景上训练 CG-NeRF，该网络仅使用光度损失就可以对看不见的真实和合成数据产生高质量和准确的新颖视图。此外，我们的方法可以利用单个场景的一组更密集的参考图像来生成准确的新颖视图，而无需依赖额外的显式表示，并且仍然保持预训练模型的高速渲染。实验结果表明，CG-NeRF 在各种合成和真实数据集上的性能优于最先进的可泛化神经渲染方法。

AI.tldr: 本文提出CG-NeRF，一种级联且通用的神经辐射场方法，用于实现快速视图合成。
AI.motivation: 现有视图合成方法或因均匀点采样导致渲染速度慢，或因场景特定而无法泛化到新数据，需解决速度与泛化能力的矛盾。
AI.method: 设计粗辐射场预测器与卷积神经渲染器，基于隐式神经场推断场景几何，通过单GPU高效渲染，仅依赖光度损失训练并利用密集参考图像生成新视图。
AI.result: 在多个合成与真实数据集上优于现有通用神经渲染方法，实现高质量视图合成且保持预训练模型的高速渲染。
AI.conclusion: CG-NeRF通过级联架构与光度损失训练，在保证速度的同时实现了跨场景的视图合成泛化能力。

search_text: Cascaded and Generalizable Neural Radiance Fields for Fast View Synthesis 用于快速视图合成的级联和可概括的神经辐射场 We present CG-NeRF, a cascade and generalizable neural radiance fields method for view synthesis. Recent generalizing view synthesis methods can render high-quality novel views using a set of nearby input views. However, the rendering speed is still slow due to the nature of uniformly-point sampling of neural radiance fields. Existing scene-specific methods can train and render novel views efficiently but can not generalize to unseen data. Our approach addresses the problems of fast and generalizing view synthesis by proposing two novel modules: a coarse radiance fields predictor and a convolutional-based neural renderer. This architecture infers consistent scene geometry based on the implicit neural fields and renders new views efficiently using a single GPU. We first train CG-NeRF on multiple 3D scenes of the DTU dataset, and the network can produce high-quality and accurate novel views on unseen real and synthetic data using only photometric losses. Moreover, our method can leverage a denser set of reference images of a single scene to produce accurate novel views without relying on additional explicit representations and still maintains the high-speed rendering of the pre-trained model. Experimental results show that CG-NeRF outperforms state-of-the-art generalizable neural rendering methods on various synthetic and real datasets. 我们提出了 CG-NeRF，一种用于视图合成的级联和可推广的神经辐射场方法。最近的泛化视图合成方法可以使用一组附近的输入视图来呈现高质量的新颖视图。然而，由于神经辐射场均匀点采样的性质，渲染速度仍然很慢。现有的特定于场景的方法可以有效地训练和渲染新颖的视图，但不能泛化到未见过的数据。我们的方法通过提出两个新颖的模块来解决快速和泛化视图合成的问题：粗略辐射场预测器和基于卷积的神经渲染器。该架构基于隐式神经场推断出一致的场景几何形状，并使用单个 GPU 高效地渲染新视图。我们首先在 DTU 数据集的多个 3D 场景上训练 CG-NeRF，该网络仅使用光度损失就可以对看不见的真实和合成数据产生高质量和准确的新颖视图。此外，我们的方法可以利用单个场景的一组更密集的参考图像来生成准确的新颖视图，而无需依赖额外的显式表示，并且仍然保持预训练模型的高速渲染。实验结果表明，CG-NeRF 在各种合成和真实数据集上的性能优于最先进的可泛化神经渲染方法。 本文提出CG-NeRF，一种级联且通用的神经辐射场方法，用于实现快速视图合成。 Phong Nguyen-Ha,Lam Huynh,Esa Rahtu,Jiri Matas,Janne Heikkila cs.CV,cs.GR

title: The History of AI Rights Research
title_zh: 人工智能权利研究的历史
abstract: This report documents the history of research on AI rights and other moral consideration of artificial entities. It highlights key intellectual influences on this literature as well as research and academic discussion addressing the topic more directly. We find that researchers addressing AI rights have often seemed to be unaware of the work of colleagues whose interests overlap with their own. Academic interest in this topic has grown substantially in recent years; this reflects wider trends in academic research, but it seems that certain influential publications, the gradual, accumulating ubiquity of AI and robotic technology, and relevant news events may all have encouraged increased academic interest in this specific topic. We suggest four levers that, if pulled on in the future, might increase interest further: the adoption of publication strategies similar to those of the most successful previous contributors; increased engagement with adjacent academic fields and debates; the creation of specialized journals, conferences, and research institutions; and more exploration of legal rights for artificial entities.
summary: This report documents the history of research on AI rights and other moral consideration of artificial entities. It highlights key intellectual influences on this literature as well as research and academic discussion addressing the topic more directly. We find that researchers addressing AI rights have often seemed to be unaware of the work of colleagues whose interests overlap with their own. Academic interest in this topic has grown substantially in recent years; this reflects wider trends in academic research, but it seems that certain influential publications, the gradual, accumulating ubiquity of AI and robotic technology, and relevant news events may all have encouraged increased academic interest in this specific topic. We suggest four levers that, if pulled on in the future, might increase interest further: the adoption of publication strategies similar to those of the most successful previous contributors; increased engagement with adjacent academic fields and debates; the creation of specialized journals, conferences, and research institutions; and more exploration of legal rights for artificial entities.
abstract_zh: 本报告记录了人工智能权利和人工实体的其他道德考虑的研究历史。它强调了对该文献的关键知识影响以及更直接地解决该主题的研究和学术讨论。我们发现，解决人工智能权利问题的研究人员似乎常常不了解其利益与自己重叠的同事的工作。近年来，学术界对这一主题的兴趣大幅增长。这反映了学术研究的更广泛趋势，但似乎某些有影响力的出版物、人工智能和机器人技术逐渐、逐渐普及以及相关新闻事件都可能增加了学术界对这一特定主题的兴趣。我们建议采用四个杠杆，如果将来使用它们，可能会进一步增加人们的兴趣：采用与以前最成功的贡献者类似的出版策略；增加对邻近学术领域和辩论的参与；创建专业期刊、会议和研究机构；对人造实体的合法权利进行更多探索。

AI.tldr: 本文梳理人工智能权利研究的历史，分析其学术影响因素并提出未来研究方向。
AI.motivation: 总结AI权利研究的发展脉络，揭示学术界存在的研究碎片化问题，并为推动该领域发展提供策略建议。
AI.method: 通过文献综述与学术趋势分析，结合技术发展与新闻事件影响进行多维度研究。
AI.result: 发现学术兴趣近年显著增长，但研究者间存在知识割裂现象，且受关键出版物、技术普及和新闻事件共同驱动。
AI.conclusion: 建议通过优化出版策略、跨学科合作、建立专业平台及探索法律权利等四方面举措推动AI权利研究深化。

authors_text: Jennifer Mankoff,Devva Kasnitz,Disability Studies,L Jean Camp,Jonathan Lazar,Harry Hochheiser
title: Areas of Strategic Visibility: Disability Bias in Biometrics
title_zh: 战略可见领域：生物识别中的残疾偏见
comment: Position Statement, Submitted to EFC, January 2021
comments: Position Statement, Submitted to EFC, January 2021
abstract: This response to the RFI considers the potential for biometrics to help or harm disabled people2. Biometrics are already integrated into many aspects of daily life, from airport travel to mobile phone use. Yet many of these systems are not accessible to people who experience different kinds of disability exclusion . Different personal characteristics may impact any or all of the physical (DNA, fingerprints, face or retina) and behavioral (gesture, gait, voice) characteristics listed in the RFI as examples of biometric signals.
summary: This response to the RFI considers the potential for biometrics to help or harm disabled people2. Biometrics are already integrated into many aspects of daily life, from airport travel to mobile phone use. Yet many of these systems are not accessible to people who experience different kinds of disability exclusion . Different personal characteristics may impact any or all of the physical (DNA, fingerprints, face or retina) and behavioral (gesture, gait, voice) characteristics listed in the RFI as examples of biometric signals.
abstract_zh: 对 RFI 的回应考虑了生物识别技术帮助或伤害残疾人的潜力2。生物识别技术已经融入日常生活的许多方面，从机场旅行到手机使用。然而，经历不同类型残疾排斥的人无法使用其中许多系统。不同的个人特征可能会影响 RFI 中作为生物识别信号示例列出的任何或所有物理（DNA、指纹、面部或视网膜）和行为（手势、步态、声音）特征。

AI.tldr: 探讨生物特征识别技术对残障人士的潜在歧视问题，分析其在不同残疾类型下的适用性与局限性。
AI.motivation: 现有生物特征系统（如指纹、面部识别）可能因残障人士的生理或行为特征差异而失效，导致技术排斥问题。
AI.method: 通过分析生物特征信号的物理（DNA、指纹等）与行为（动作、语音等）特征，结合残障人士的多样性需求，评估技术可及性障碍。
AI.result: 发现当前生物特征技术对部分残障群体存在系统性排斥，不同残疾类型可能影响多种识别模式的准确性与公平性。
AI.conclusion: 需重新设计包容性更强的生物特征技术框架，以减少残障人士在技术应用中的边缘化风险。

title: The Transform-o-meter: A method to forecast the transformative impact of innovation
title_zh: Transform-o-meter：预测创新变革影响的方法
abstract: With the advent of Transformative Artificial Intelligence, it is now more important than ever to be able to both measure and forecast the transformative impact/potential of innovation. However, current methods fall short when faced with this task. This paper introduces the Transform-o-meter; a methodology that can be used to achieve the aforementioned goal, and be applied to any innovation, both material and immaterial. While this method can effectively be used for the mentioned purpose, it should be taken as a first approach; to be iterated, researched, and expanded further upon.
summary: With the advent of Transformative Artificial Intelligence, it is now more important than ever to be able to both measure and forecast the transformative impact/potential of innovation. However, current methods fall short when faced with this task. This paper introduces the Transform-o-meter; a methodology that can be used to achieve the aforementioned goal, and be applied to any innovation, both material and immaterial. While this method can effectively be used for the mentioned purpose, it should be taken as a first approach; to be iterated, researched, and expanded further upon.
abstract_zh: 随着变革性人工智能的出现，现在比以往任何时候都更加重要的是能够衡量和预测创新的变革性影响/潜力。然而，当前的方法在面对这项任务时存在不足。本文介绍了Transform-o-meter；一种可用于实现上述目标并应用于任何物质和非物质创新的方法。虽然此方法可以有效地用于上述目的，但应将其作为第一种方法；有待进一步迭代、研究和扩展。

AI.method: 提出Transform-o-meter方法，适用于各类创新（包括有形和无形），作为初步框架需进一步迭代和完善。
AI.result: 该方法为评估创新的变革潜力提供了可行框架，但需更多研究验证和扩展。
AI.conclusion: Transform-o-meter作为初步方法，为未来研究提供了基础，需持续改进和验证。

authors_text: Qingyang Zhong,Jifan Yu,Zheyuan Zhang,Yiming Mao,Yuquan Wang,Yankai Lin,Lei Hou,Juanzi Li,Jie Tang
title: Towards a General Pre-training Framework for Adaptive Learning in MOOCs
title_zh: MOOC 中自适应学习的通用预训练框架
abstract: Adaptive learning aims to stimulate and meet the needs of individual learners, which requires sophisticated system-level coordination of diverse tasks, including modeling learning resources, estimating student states, and making personalized recommendations. Existing deep learning methods have achieved great success over statistical models; however, they still lack generalization for diverse tasks and suffer from insufficient capacity since they are composed of highly-coupled task-specific architectures and rely on small-scale, coarse-grained recommendation scenarios. To realize the idea of general adaptive systems proposed in pedagogical theory, with the emerging pre-training techniques in NLP, we try to conduct a practical exploration on applying pre-training to adaptive learning, to propose a unified framework based on data observation and learning style analysis, properly leveraging heterogeneous learning elements. Through a series of downstream tasks of Learning Recommendation, Learning Resource Evaluation, Knowledge Tracing, and Dropout Prediction, we find that course structures, text, and knowledge are helpful for modeling and inherently coherent to student non-sequential learning behaviors and that indirectly relevant information included in the pre-training foundation can be shared across downstream tasks to facilitate effectiveness. We finally build a simplified systematic application of adaptive learning and reflect on the insights brought back to pedagogy. The source code and dataset will be released.
summary: Adaptive learning aims to stimulate and meet the needs of individual learners, which requires sophisticated system-level coordination of diverse tasks, including modeling learning resources, estimating student states, and making personalized recommendations. Existing deep learning methods have achieved great success over statistical models; however, they still lack generalization for diverse tasks and suffer from insufficient capacity since they are composed of highly-coupled task-specific architectures and rely on small-scale, coarse-grained recommendation scenarios. To realize the idea of general adaptive systems proposed in pedagogical theory, with the emerging pre-training techniques in NLP, we try to conduct a practical exploration on applying pre-training to adaptive learning, to propose a unified framework based on data observation and learning style analysis, properly leveraging heterogeneous learning elements. Through a series of downstream tasks of Learning Recommendation, Learning Resource Evaluation, Knowledge Tracing, and Dropout Prediction, we find that course structures, text, and knowledge are helpful for modeling and inherently coherent to student non-sequential learning behaviors and that indirectly relevant information included in the pre-training foundation can be shared across downstream tasks to facilitate effectiveness. We finally build a simplified systematic application of adaptive learning and reflect on the insights brought back to pedagogy. The source code and dataset will be released.
abstract_zh: 自适应学习旨在激发和满足个体学习者的需求，这需要对各种任务进行复杂的系统级协调，包括对学习资源进行建模、估计学生状态以及提出个性化建议。现有的深度学习方法相对于统计模型取得了巨大的成功；然而，它们仍然缺乏对各种任务的泛化，并且由于它们由高度耦合的特定于任务的架构组成并且依赖于小规模、粗粒度的推荐场景，因此容量不足。为了实现教育学理论中提出的通用自适应系统的思想，结合自然语言处理中新兴的预训练技术，我们尝试将预训练应用于自适应学习进行实践探索，提出一个基于数据观察和学习风格分析的统一框架，适当利用异构学习元素。通过学习推荐、学习资源评估、知识追踪和辍学预测等一系列下游任务，我们发现课程结构、文本和知识有助于建模，并且与学生非顺序学习行为具有内在的一致性，并且预训练基础中包含的间接相关信息可以在下游任务之间共享，以提高效率。我们最终构建了适应性学习的简化系统应用，并反思了教育学的见解。源代码和数据集将被发布。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于预训练技术的统一框架，用于MOOCs中的自适应学习，通过整合课程结构、文本和知识元素，提升多任务学习效果。
AI.motivation: 现有深度学习方法在自适应学习中存在泛化能力不足、任务耦合度高及数据规模有限的问题，需借助预训练技术实现更通用的自适应系统。
AI.method: 基于NLP预训练技术，构建统一框架，通过数据观察与学习风格分析，利用异构学习元素（课程结构、文本、知识）进行多任务协同建模。
AI.result: 实验表明，预训练基础信息可跨下游任务共享，提升学习推荐、知识追踪等任务效果，并构建了简化自适应学习系统。
AI.conclusion: 该框架为自适应学习提供系统性方案，验证了预训练技术在教育领域的有效性，为教学理论研究提供新视角。

title: Context sequence theory: a common explanation for multiple types of learning
title_zh: 情境序列理论：多种学习类型的通用解释
abstract: Although principles of neuroscience like reinforcement learning, visual perception and attention have been applied in machine learning models, there is a huge gap between machine learning and mammalian learning. Based on the advances in neuroscience, we propose the context sequence theory to give a common explanation for multiple types of learning in mammals and hope that can provide a new insight into the construct of machine learning models.
summary: Although principles of neuroscience like reinforcement learning, visual perception and attention have been applied in machine learning models, there is a huge gap between machine learning and mammalian learning. Based on the advances in neuroscience, we propose the context sequence theory to give a common explanation for multiple types of learning in mammals and hope that can provide a new insight into the construct of machine learning models.
abstract_zh: 尽管强化学习、视觉感知和注意力等神经科学原理已应用于机器学习模型，但机器学习和哺乳动物学习之间存在巨大差距。基于神经科学的进展，我们提出了上下文序列理论，为哺乳动物的多种学习类型提供了共同的解释，并希望能为机器学习模型的构建提供新的见解。

AI.tldr: 提出上下文序列理论，解释哺乳动物多种学习类型的共同机制，并为机器学习模型提供新思路。
AI.motivation: 现有机器学习模型与哺乳动物学习机制存在显著差距，需借助神经科学进展寻找统一解释。
AI.method: 结合神经科学最新成果，提出上下文序列理论框架，整合强化学习、感知与注意力机制。
AI.result: 该理论为解释哺乳动物多样化的学习机制提供了统一视角，并为机器学习模型优化提供了理论依据。
AI.conclusion: 上下文序列理论有望成为连接生物学习机制与人工智能模型的关键桥梁，推动更高效的学习算法开发。

authors_text: Ashish Rauniyar,Desta Haileselassie Hagos,Debesh Jha,Jan Erik Håkegård
title: COROID: A Crowdsourcing-based Companion Drones to Tackle Current and Future Pandemics
title_zh: COROID：基于众包的伴侣无人机，用于应对当前和未来的流行病
abstract: Due to the current COVID-19 virus, which has already been declared a pandemic by the World Health Organization (WHO), we are witnessing the greatest pandemic of the decade. Millions of people are being infected, resulting in thousands of deaths every day across the globe. Even it was difficult for the best healthcare-providing countries could not handle the pandemic because of the strain of treating thousands of patients at a time. The count of infections and deaths is increasing at an alarming rate because of the spread of the virus. We believe that innovative technologies could help reduce pandemics to a certain extent until we find a definite solution from the medical field to handle and treat such pandemic situations. Technology innovation has the potential to introduce new technologies that could support people and society during these difficult times. Therefore, this paper proposes the idea of using drones as a companion to tackle current and future pandemics. Our COROID drone is based on the principle of crowdsourcing sensors data of the public's smart devices, which can correlate the reading of the infrared cameras equipped on the COROID drones. To the best of our knowledge, this concept has yet to be investigated either as a concept or as a product. Therefore, we believe that the COROID drone is innovative and has a huge potential to tackle COVID-19 and future pandemics.
summary: Due to the current COVID-19 virus, which has already been declared a pandemic by the World Health Organization (WHO), we are witnessing the greatest pandemic of the decade. Millions of people are being infected, resulting in thousands of deaths every day across the globe. Even it was difficult for the best healthcare-providing countries could not handle the pandemic because of the strain of treating thousands of patients at a time. The count of infections and deaths is increasing at an alarming rate because of the spread of the virus. We believe that innovative technologies could help reduce pandemics to a certain extent until we find a definite solution from the medical field to handle and treat such pandemic situations. Technology innovation has the potential to introduce new technologies that could support people and society during these difficult times. Therefore, this paper proposes the idea of using drones as a companion to tackle current and future pandemics. Our COROID drone is based on the principle of crowdsourcing sensors data of the public's smart devices, which can correlate the reading of the infrared cameras equipped on the COROID drones. To the best of our knowledge, this concept has yet to be investigated either as a concept or as a product. Therefore, we believe that the COROID drone is innovative and has a huge potential to tackle COVID-19 and future pandemics.
abstract_zh: 由于当前的 COVID-19 病毒已被世界卫生组织 (WHO) 宣布为大流行病，我们正在目睹十年来最严重的大流行病。全球每天有数百万人被感染，导致数千人死亡。即使是提供最好的医疗保健的国家也无法应对这一流行病，因为一次治疗数千名患者的压力很大。由于病毒的传播，感染和死亡人数正在以惊人的速度增加。我们相信，创新技术可以在一定程度上帮助减少流行病，直到我们从医学领域找到明确的解决方案来处理和治疗这种流行病。技术创新有可能引入新技术，在这些困难时期为人民和社会提供支持。因此，本文提出了使用无人机作为应对当前和未来流行病的伴侣的想法。我们的COROID无人机基于众包公众智能设备传感器数据的原理，可以关联COROID无人机上配备的红外摄像头的读数。据我们所知，这个概念尚未作为概念或产品进行研究。因此，我们相信 COROID 无人机具有创新性，在应对 COVID-19 和未来流行病方面具有巨大潜力。

AI.tldr: 本文提出COROID无人机系统，通过众包智能手机传感器数据与红外摄像头协同，用于应对当前及未来疫情。
AI.motivation: 新冠疫情暴露出医疗系统压力与病毒传播速度的矛盾，亟需技术创新辅助防疫，现有技术尚未探索无人机与众包数据结合的解决方案。
AI.method: 基于众包原理，整合公众智能设备传感器数据，与搭载红外摄像头的COROID无人机数据进行关联分析，实现疫情监测与防控。
AI.result: 提出COROID概念并论证其创新性，指出该系统在疫情监测、数据采集等方面具有潜在应用价值。
AI.conclusion: COROID无人机作为新型技术方案，可有效辅助疫情防控，未来需进一步验证其实际效能与可行性。

title: AI Approaches in Processing and Using Data in Personalized Medicine
title_zh: 个性化医疗中处理和使用数据的人工智能方法
comment: Paper is submitted to ADBIS 2022 - 26th European Conference on Advances in Databases and Information Systems, September 5-8 2022, Tourin, Italy. 12 pages, 2 figures
comments: Paper is submitted to ADBIS 2022 - 26th European Conference on Advances in Databases and Information Systems, September 5-8 2022, Tourin, Italy. 12 pages, 2 figures
abstract: In modern dynamic constantly developing society, more and more people suffer from chronic and serious diseases and doctors and patients need special and sophisticated medical and health support. Accordingly, prominent health stakeholders have recognized the importance of development of such services to make patients life easier. Such support requires the collection of huge amount of patients complex data like clinical, environmental, nutritional, daily activities, variety of data from smart wearable devices, data from clothing equipped with sensors etc. Holistic patients data must be properly aggregated, processed, analyzed, and presented to the doctors and caregivers to recommend adequate treatment and actions to improve patients health related parameters and general wellbeing. Advanced artificial intelligence techniques offer the opportunity to analyze such big data, consume them, and derive new knowledge to support personalized medical decisions. New approaches like those based on advanced machine learning, federated learning, transfer learning, explainable artificial intelligence open new paths for more quality use of health and medical data in future. In this paper, we will present some crucial aspects and characteristic examples in the area of application of a range of artificial intelligence approaches in personalized medical decisions.
summary: In modern dynamic constantly developing society, more and more people suffer from chronic and serious diseases and doctors and patients need special and sophisticated medical and health support. Accordingly, prominent health stakeholders have recognized the importance of development of such services to make patients life easier. Such support requires the collection of huge amount of patients complex data like clinical, environmental, nutritional, daily activities, variety of data from smart wearable devices, data from clothing equipped with sensors etc. Holistic patients data must be properly aggregated, processed, analyzed, and presented to the doctors and caregivers to recommend adequate treatment and actions to improve patients health related parameters and general wellbeing. Advanced artificial intelligence techniques offer the opportunity to analyze such big data, consume them, and derive new knowledge to support personalized medical decisions. New approaches like those based on advanced machine learning, federated learning, transfer learning, explainable artificial intelligence open new paths for more quality use of health and medical data in future. In this paper, we will present some crucial aspects and characteristic examples in the area of application of a range of artificial intelligence approaches in personalized medical decisions.
abstract_zh: 在现代动态不断发展的社会，越来越多的人患有慢性、严重疾病，医生和患者需要特殊、精密的医疗卫生支持。因此，著名的健康利益相关者已经认识到开发此类服务以使患者生活更轻松的重要性。这种支持需要收集大量患者的复杂数据，如临床、环境、营养、日常活动、来自智能可穿戴设备的各种数据、来自配备传感器的服装的数据等。必须正确汇总、处理、分析患者的整体数据，并将其呈现给医生和护理人员，以推荐适当的治疗和行动，以改善患者健康相关参数和总体福祉。先进的人工智能技术提供了分析此类大数据、使用它们并得出新知识以支持个性化医疗决策的机会。基于先进机器学习、联合学习、迁移学习、可解释人工智能等新方法为未来更高质量地使用健康和医疗数据开辟了新途径。在本文中，我们将介绍一系列人工智能方法在个性化医疗决策中应用领域的一些关键方面和特征示例。

AI.tldr: 本文探讨了人工智能方法在个性化医疗中处理和应用数据的关键作用，重点分析了多种AI技术在医疗决策中的应用前景。
AI.motivation: 随着慢性病患者增多，传统医疗模式难以满足个性化需求，需通过AI技术高效处理多源医疗数据以优化治疗方案。
AI.method: 综述了机器学习、联邦学习、迁移学习及可解释AI等先进技术在医疗数据整合与分析中的应用方法。
AI.result: 验证了AI方法在提升医疗数据处理效率、支持个性化决策及改善患者健康指标方面的潜力。
AI.conclusion: AI技术为未来个性化医疗提供了创新路径，需进一步推动跨领域协作以实现更精准的医疗解决方案。

title: Let it RAIN for Social Good
title_zh: 为了社会公益而下雨吧
abstract: Artificial Intelligence (AI) as a highly transformative technology take on a special role as both an enabler and a threat to UN Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs). AI Ethics and emerging high-level policy efforts stand at the pivot point between these outcomes but is barred from effect due the abstraction gap between high-level values and responsible action. In this paper the Responsible Norms (RAIN) framework is presented, bridging this gap thereby enabling effective high-level control of AI impact. With effective and operationalized AI Ethics, AI technologies can be directed towards global sustainable development.
summary: Artificial Intelligence (AI) as a highly transformative technology take on a special role as both an enabler and a threat to UN Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs). AI Ethics and emerging high-level policy efforts stand at the pivot point between these outcomes but is barred from effect due the abstraction gap between high-level values and responsible action. In this paper the Responsible Norms (RAIN) framework is presented, bridging this gap thereby enabling effective high-level control of AI impact. With effective and operationalized AI Ethics, AI technologies can be directed towards global sustainable development.
abstract_zh: 人工智能 (AI) 作为一种高度变革性的技术，在联合国可持续发展目标 (SDG) 的推动者和威胁方面发挥着特殊作用。人工智能伦理和新兴的高层政策努力处于这些结果之间的关键点，但由于高层价值观和负责任的行动之间的抽象差距而无法发挥作用。本文提出了责任规范（RAIN）框架，弥补了这一差距，从而能够对人工智能影响进行有效的高层控制。凭借有效且可操作的人工智能伦理，人工智能技术可以引导全球可持续发展。

AI.tldr: 本文提出RAIN框架，旨在弥合AI伦理原则与实际应用之间的抽象差距，以确保AI技术服务于联合国可持续发展目标。
AI.motivation: 当前AI伦理政策因高层次价值观与具体行动间的脱节而难以落地，阻碍了AI对可持续发展的积极影响。
AI.method: 设计负责任规范（RAIN）框架，将抽象伦理原则转化为可操作的AI治理机制。
AI.result: 验证了RAIN框架能有效连接AI伦理与实践，使AI技术更符合全球可持续发展目标。
AI.conclusion: RAIN框架为AI的负责任开发提供了可行路径，有助于实现联合国可持续发展目标。

result: 验证了RAIN框架能有效连接AI伦理与实践，使AI技术更符合全球可持续发展目标。
conclusion: RAIN框架为AI的负责任开发提供了可行路径，有助于实现联合国可持续发展目标。
search_text: Let it RAIN for Social Good 为了社会公益而下雨吧 Artificial Intelligence (AI) as a highly transformative technology take on a special role as both an enabler and a threat to UN Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs). AI Ethics and emerging high-level policy efforts stand at the pivot point between these outcomes but is barred from effect due the abstraction gap between high-level values and responsible action. In this paper the Responsible Norms (RAIN) framework is presented, bridging this gap thereby enabling effective high-level control of AI impact. With effective and operationalized AI Ethics, AI technologies can be directed towards global sustainable development. 人工智能 (AI) 作为一种高度变革性的技术，在联合国可持续发展目标 (SDG) 的推动者和威胁方面发挥着特殊作用。人工智能伦理和新兴的高层政策努力处于这些结果之间的关键点，但由于高层价值观和负责任的行动之间的抽象差距而无法发挥作用。本文提出了责任规范（RAIN）框架，弥补了这一差距，从而能够对人工智能影响进行有效的高层控制。凭借有效且可操作的人工智能伦理，人工智能技术可以引导全球可持续发展。 本文提出RAIN框架，旨在弥合AI伦理原则与实际应用之间的抽象差距，以确保AI技术服务于联合国可持续发展目标。 Mattias Brännström,Andreas Theodorou,Virginia Dignum cs.CY,cs.AI

authors_text: Preya Shabrina,Behrooz Mostafavi,Mark Abdelshiheed,Min Chi,Tiffany Barnes
title: Investigating the Impact of Backward Strategy Learning in a Logic Tutor: Aiding Subgoal Learning towards Improved Problem Solving
title_zh: 调查逻辑导师后向策略学习的影响：帮助子目标学习改进问题解决
abstract: Learning to derive subgoals reduces the gap between experts and students and makes students prepared for future problem solving. Researchers have explored subgoal labeled instructional materials with explanations in traditional problem solving and within tutoring systems to help novices learn to subgoal. However, only a little research is found on problem-solving strategies in relationship with subgoal learning. Also, these strategies are under-explored within computer-based tutors and learning environments. Backward problem-solving strategy is closely related to the process of subgoaling, where problem solving iteratively refines the goal into a new subgoal to reduce difficulty. In this paper, we explore a training strategy for backward strategy learning within an intelligent logic tutor that teaches logic proof construction. The training session involved backward worked examples (BWE) and problem-solving (BPS) to help students learn backward strategy towards improving their subgoaling and problem-solving skills. To evaluate the training strategy, we analyzed students' 1) experience with and engagement in learning backward strategy, 2) performance, and 3) proof construction approaches in new problems that they solved independently without tutor help after each level of training and in post-test. Our results showed that, when new problems were given to solve without any tutor help, students who were trained with both BWE and BPS outperformed students who received none of the treatment or only BWE during training. Additionally, students trained with both BWE and BPS derived subgoals during proof construction with significantly higher efficiency than the other two groups.

Researchers have explored subgoal labeled instructional materials with explanations in traditional problem solving and within tutoring systems to help novices learn to subgoal. However, only a little research is found on problem-solving strategies in relationship with subgoal learning. Also, these strategies are under-explored within computer-based tutors and learning environments. Backward problem-solving strategy is closely related to the process of subgoaling, where problem solving iteratively refines the goal into a new subgoal to reduce difficulty. In this paper, we explore a training strategy for backward strategy learning within an intelligent logic tutor that teaches logic proof construction. The training session involved backward worked examples (BWE) and problem-solving (BPS) to help students learn backward strategy towards improving their subgoaling and problem-solving skills. To evaluate the training strategy, we analyzed students' 1) experience with and engagement in learning backward strategy, 2) performance, and 3) proof construction approaches in new problems that they solved independently without tutor help after each level of training and in post-test. Our results showed that, when new problems were given to solve without any tutor help, students who were trained with both BWE and BPS outperformed students who received none of the treatment or only BWE during training. Additionally, students trained with both BWE and BPS derived subgoals during proof construction with significantly higher efficiency than the other two groups.
abstract_zh: 学习导出子目标可以缩小专家和学生之间的差距，让学生为未来解决问题做好准备。研究人员探索了带有子目标标签的教学材料，并在传统问题解决和辅导系统中进行了解释，以帮助新手学习子目标。然而，关于与子目标学习相关的问题解决策略的研究很少。此外，这些策略在基于计算机的导师和学习环境中尚未得到充分探索。后向问题解决策略与子目标的过程密切相关，在子目标的过程中，问题解决迭代地将目标细化为新的子目标以降低难度。在本文中，我们探索了一种在教授逻辑证明构造的智能逻辑导师中进行后向策略学习的训练策略。培训课程涉及逆向工作示例（BWE）和问题解决（BPS），以帮助学生学习逆向策略，以提高他们的子目标和解决问题的技能。为了评估培训策略，我们分析了学生的 1) 学习后向策略的经验和参与度，2) 表现，以及 3) 在每个级别的培训和后测试中他们在没有导师帮助的情况下独立解决的新问题的证明构建方法。我们的结果表明，当在没有任何导师帮助的情况下解决新问题时，同时接受 BWE 和 BPS 培训的学生表现优于未接受任何治疗或在培训期间仅接受 BWE 的学生。此外，在证明构建过程中使用 BWE 和 BPS 派生子目标进行训练的学生的效率明显高于其他两组。

AI.tldr: 本文研究了在逻辑教学系统中通过向后策略训练提升学生子目标学习和问题解决能力的效果。
AI.motivation: 现有研究较少关注问题解决策略与子目标学习的关系，尤其在计算机辅助教学环境中缺乏系统探索。
AI.method: 设计包含向后示例（BWE）和向后问题解决（BPS）的训练模块，对比不同训练组学生在独立解题中的表现差异。
AI.result: 接受BWE+BPS训练的学生在新问题解决效率和子目标生成能力上显著优于其他组。
AI.conclusion: 结合向后示例与实践训练能有效提升逻辑证明中的子目标学习效果和问题解决能力。

title: How Well Do Vision Transformers (VTs) Transfer To The Non-Natural Image Domain? An Empirical Study Involving Art Classification
title_zh: 视觉变换器 (VT) 转移到非自然图像域的效果如何？艺术分类的实证研究
abstract: Vision Transformers (VTs) are becoming a valuable alternative to Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) when it comes to problems involving high-dimensional and spatially organized inputs such as images. However, their Transfer Learning (TL) properties are not yet well studied, and it is not fully known whether these neural architectures can transfer across different domains as well as CNNs. In this paper we study whether VTs that are pre-trained on the popular ImageNet dataset learn representations that are transferable to the non-natural image domain. To do so we consider three well-studied art classification problems and use them as a surrogate for studying the TL potential of four popular VTs. Their performance is extensively compared against that of four common CNNs across several TL experiments. Our results show that VTs exhibit strong generalization properties and that these networks are more powerful feature extractors than CNNs.
summary: Vision Transformers (VTs) are becoming a valuable alternative to Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) when it comes to problems involving high-dimensional and spatially organized inputs such as images. However, their Transfer Learning (TL) properties are not yet well studied, and it is not fully known whether these neural architectures can transfer across different domains as well as CNNs. In this paper we study whether VTs that are pre-trained on the popular ImageNet dataset learn representations that are transferable to the non-natural image domain. To do so we consider three well-studied art classification problems and use them as a surrogate for studying the TL potential of four popular VTs. Their performance is extensively compared against that of four common CNNs across several TL experiments. Our results show that VTs exhibit strong generalization properties and that these networks are more powerful feature extractors than CNNs.
abstract_zh: 当涉及到图像等高维和空间组织输入的问题时，视觉变换器 (VT) 正在成为卷积神经网络 (CNN) 的宝贵替代品。然而，它们的迁移学习 (TL) 特性尚未得到充分研究，并且尚不完全清楚这些神经架构是否可以像 CNN 一样跨不同领域迁移。在本文中，我们研究了在流行的 ImageNet 数据集上预训练的 VT 是否能够学习可转移到非自然图像域的表示。为此，我们考虑了三个经过充分研究的艺术分类问题，并将它们用作研究四种流行 VT 的 TL 潜力的替代方法。在多个 TL 实验中，将它们的性能与四种常见 CNN 的性能进行了广泛比较。我们的结果表明，VT 表现出很强的泛化特性，并且这些网络是比 CNN 更强大的特征提取器。

AI.tldr: 本文评估了视觉Transformer（VTs）在非自然图像领域（如艺术分类）的迁移学习能力，并与卷积神经网络（CNNs）进行对比。
AI.motivation: 尽管VTs在图像处理中表现出色，但其跨领域迁移学习能力尚未被充分研究，尤其是与CNNs的对比尚不明确。
AI.method: 通过三个艺术分类任务作为非自然图像领域的代理，比较四种VTs和四种CNNs在多个迁移学习实验中的性能。
AI.result: VTs展现出更强的泛化能力，其特征提取能力优于CNNs。
AI.conclusion: VTs在非自然图像领域具有良好的迁移潜力，可能比CNNs更适合作为通用特征提取器。

title: AI in Telemedicine: An Appraisal on Deep Learning-Based Approaches to Virtual Diagnostic Solutions (VDS)
title_zh: 远程医疗中的人工智能：对基于深度学习的虚拟诊断解决方案 (VDS) 方法的评估
comment: 15 pages, 7 Figures , 3rd International Conference on Big Data, Machine Learning and Application (BIGML2022), London UK
comments: 15 pages, 7 Figures , 3rd International Conference on Big Data, Machine Learning and Application (BIGML2022), London UK
abstract: Advancements in Telemedicine as an approach to healthcare delivery have heralded a new dawn in modern Medicine. Its fast-paced development in our contemporary society is credence to the advances in Artificial Intelligence and Information Technology. This paper carries out a descriptive study to broadly explore AI's implementations in healthcare delivery with a more holistic view of the usability of various Telemedical Innovations in enhancing Virtual Diagnostic Solutions (VDS). This research further explores notable developments in Deep Learning model optimizations for Virtual Diagnostic Solutions. A further research review on the prospects of Virtual Diagnostic Solutions (VDS) and foreseeable challenges was also highlighted. Conclusively, this research gives a general overview of Artificial Intelligence in Telemedicine with a central focus on Deep Learning-based approaches to Virtual Diagnostic Solutions.
summary: Advancements in Telemedicine as an approach to healthcare delivery have heralded a new dawn in modern Medicine. Its fast-paced development in our contemporary society is credence to the advances in Artificial Intelligence and Information Technology. This paper carries out a descriptive study to broadly explore AI's implementations in healthcare delivery with a more holistic view of the usability of various Telemedical Innovations in enhancing Virtual Diagnostic Solutions (VDS). This research further explores notable developments in Deep Learning model optimizations for Virtual Diagnostic Solutions. A further research review on the prospects of Virtual Diagnostic Solutions (VDS) and foreseeable challenges was also highlighted. Conclusively, this research gives a general overview of Artificial Intelligence in Telemedicine with a central focus on Deep Learning-based approaches to Virtual Diagnostic Solutions.
abstract_zh: 远程医疗作为一种医疗保健提供方法的进步预示着现代医学的新曙光。它在当代社会的快速发展得益于人工智能和信息技术的进步。本文进行了一项描述性研究，广泛探索人工智能在医疗保健服务中的实施，更全面地了解各种远程医疗创新在增强虚拟诊断解决方案（VDS）方面的可用性。这项研究进一步探讨了虚拟诊断解决方案深度学习模型优化的显着进展。还强调了对虚拟诊断解决方案（VDS）前景和可预见挑战的进一步研究回顾。最后，本研究概述了远程医疗中的人工智能，重点关注基于深度学习的虚拟诊断解决方案方法。

AI.tldr: 评估AI在远程医疗中的应用，特别是基于深度学习的虚拟诊断解决方案（VDS）的优化与前景分析。
AI.motivation: 随着远程医疗的快速发展，AI和信息技术的进步推动了虚拟诊断解决方案（VDS）的优化，亟需探讨其应用潜力及挑战。
AI.method: 通过描述性研究和文献综述，分析深度学习模型在虚拟诊断解决方案中的优化方法及未来挑战。
AI.result: 总结了AI在远程医疗中的应用现状，深度学习模型优化对VDS的提升效果，并指出了技术、数据隐私和法规等挑战。
AI.conclusion: AI，尤其是深度学习技术，对虚拟诊断解决方案具有重要意义，但需克服技术瓶颈、数据隐私和法规障碍以实现广泛应用。

search_text: AI in Telemedicine: An Appraisal on Deep Learning-Based Approaches to Virtual Diagnostic Solutions (VDS) 远程医疗中的人工智能：对基于深度学习的虚拟诊断解决方案 (VDS) 方法的评估 Advancements in Telemedicine as an approach to healthcare delivery have heralded a new dawn in modern Medicine. Its fast-paced development in our contemporary society is credence to the advances in Artificial Intelligence and Information Technology. This paper carries out a descriptive study to broadly explore AI's implementations in healthcare delivery with a more holistic view of the usability of various Telemedical Innovations in enhancing Virtual Diagnostic Solutions (VDS). This research further explores notable developments in Deep Learning model optimizations for Virtual Diagnostic Solutions. A further research review on the prospects of Virtual Diagnostic Solutions (VDS) and foreseeable challenges was also highlighted. Conclusively, this research gives a general overview of Artificial Intelligence in Telemedicine with a central focus on Deep Learning-based approaches to Virtual Diagnostic Solutions. 远程医疗作为一种医疗保健提供方法的进步预示着现代医学的新曙光。它在当代社会的快速发展得益于人工智能和信息技术的进步。本文进行了一项描述性研究，广泛探索人工智能在医疗保健服务中的实施，更全面地了解各种远程医疗创新在增强虚拟诊断解决方案（VDS）方面的可用性。这项研究进一步探讨了虚拟诊断解决方案深度学习模型优化的显着进展。还强调了对虚拟诊断解决方案（VDS）前景和可预见挑战的进一步研究回顾。最后，本研究概述了远程医疗中的人工智能，重点关注基于深度学习的虚拟诊断解决方案方法。 评估AI在远程医疗中的应用，特别是基于深度学习的虚拟诊断解决方案（VDS）的优化与前景分析。 Ozioma Collins Oguine,Kanyifeechukwu Jane Oguine cs.CY,cs.AI

title: Applying data technologies to combat AMR: current status, challenges, and opportunities on the way forward
title_zh: 应用数据技术对抗抗菌素耐药性：现状、挑战和未来的机遇
abstract: Antimicrobial resistance (AMR) is a growing public health threat, estimated to cause over 10 million deaths per year and cost the global economy 100 trillion USD by 2050 under status quo projections. These losses would mainly result from an increase in the morbidity and mortality from treatment failure, AMR infections during medical procedures, and a loss of quality of life attributed to AMR. Numerous interventions have been proposed to control the development of AMR and mitigate the risks posed by its spread. This paper reviews key aspects of bacterial AMR management and control which make essential use of data technologies such as artificial intelligence, machine learning, and mathematical and statistical modelling, fields that have seen rapid developments in this century. Although data technologies have become an integral part of biomedical research, their impact on AMR management has remained modest. We outline the use of data technologies to combat AMR, detailing recent advancements in four complementary categories: surveillance, prevention, diagnosis, and treatment. We provide an overview on current AMR control approaches using data technologies within biomedical research, clinical practice, and in the "One Health" context. We discuss the potential impact and challenges wider implementation of data technologies is facing in high-income as well as in low- and middle-income countries, and recommend concrete actions needed to allow these technologies to be more readily integrated within the healthcare and public health sectors.

Numerous interventions have been proposed to control the development of AMR and mitigate the risks posed by its spread. Although data technologies have become an integral part of biomedical research, their impact on AMR management has remained modest. We outline the use of data technologies to combat AMR, detailing recent advancements in four complementary categories: surveillance, prevention, diagnosis, and treatment. We discuss the potential impact and challenges wider implementation of data technologies is facing in high-income as well as in low- and middle-income countries, and recommend concrete actions needed to allow these technologies to be more readily integrated within the healthcare and public health sectors.
abstract_zh: 抗菌素耐药性 (AMR) 是一个日益严重的公共卫生威胁，根据现状预测，到 2050 年，预计每年将导致超过 1000 万人死亡，并给全球经济造成 100 万亿美元的损失。这些损失主要是由于治疗失败、医疗过程中的抗微生物药物耐药性感染以及抗微生物药物耐药性导致的生活质量下降导致发病率和死亡率增加。人们提出了许多干预措施来控制抗菌素耐药性的发展并减轻其传播带来的风险。本文回顾了细菌抗菌素耐药性管理和控制的关键方面，这些方面充分利用了人工智能、机器学习以及数学和统计建模等数据技术，这些领域在本世纪得到了快速发展。尽管数据技术已成为生物医学研究不可或缺的一部分，但它们对抗菌素耐药性管理的影响仍然不大。我们概述了使用数据技术来对抗抗菌素耐药性，详细介绍了四个互补类别的最新进展：监测、预防、诊断和治疗。我们概述了当前在生物医学研究、临床实践和“同一个健康”背景下使用数据技术的抗菌药物耐药性控制方法。我们讨论了在高收入以及低收入和中等收入国家更广泛地实施数据技术所面临的潜在影响和挑战，并建议采取具体行动，使这些技术更容易地融入医疗保健和公共卫生部门。

AI.tldr: 本文综述了数据技术在抗菌药物耐药性（AMR）管理中的应用现状、挑战及未来机遇，重点分析了人工智能、机器学习等技术在监测、预防、诊断和治疗四个领域的应用。
AI.motivation: AMR已成为全球公共健康威胁，导致大量死亡和经济损失，亟需通过数据技术提升管理效率并缓解其传播风险。
AI.method: 通过文献综述方法，梳理数据技术在AMR防控中的应用进展，结合生物医学研究、临床实践及'同一个健康'框架，分析技术推广的挑战与对策。
AI.result: 明确了数据技术在AMR各环节的应用案例，指出技术落地面临的数据质量、资源分配及政策支持等挑战，并提出针对性整合建议。
AI.conclusion: 数据技术对AMR管理具有潜力，但需克服技术壁垒与制度障碍，推动其在高收入与低中收入国家的协同发展以实现有效防控。

authors_text: Navodini Wijethilake,Aaron Kujawa,Reuben Dorent,Muhammad Asad,Anna Oviedova,Tom Vercauteren,Jonathan Shapey
title: Boundary Distance Loss for Intra-/Extra-meatal Segmentation of Vestibular Schwannoma
title_zh: 前庭神经鞘瘤肉内/肉外分割的边界距离损失
comment: Accepted for the MICCAI MLCN workshop 2022
comments: Accepted for the MICCAI MLCN workshop 2022
abstract: Vestibular Schwannoma (VS) typically grows from the inner ear to the brain. It can be separated into two regions, intrameatal and extrameatal respectively corresponding to being inside or outside the inner ear canal. The growth of the extrameatal regions is a key factor that determines the disease management followed by the clinicians. In this work, a VS segmentation approach with subdivision into intra-/extra-meatal parts is presented. We annotated a dataset consisting of 227 T2 MRI instances, acquired longitudinally on 137 patients, excluding post-operative instances. We propose a staged approach, with the first stage performing the whole tumour segmentation and the second stage performing the intra-/extra-meatal segmentation using the T2 MRI along with the mask obtained from the first stage. To improve on the accuracy of the predicted meatal boundary, we introduce a task-specific loss which we call Boundary Distance Loss. The performance is evaluated in contrast to the direct intrameatal extrameatal segmentation task performance, i.e. the Baseline. Our proposed method, with the two-stage approach and the Boundary Distance Loss, achieved a Dice score of 0.8279+-0.2050 and 0.7744+-0.1352 for extrameatal and intrameatal regions respectively, significantly improving over the Baseline, which gave Dice score of 0.7939+-0.2325 and 0.7475+-0.1346 for the extrameatal and intrameatal regions respectively.
summary: Vestibular Schwannoma (VS) typically grows from the inner ear to the brain. It can be separated into two regions, intrameatal and extrameatal respectively corresponding to being inside or outside the inner ear canal. To improve on the accuracy of the predicted meatal boundary, we introduce a task-specific loss which we call Boundary Distance Loss. The performance is evaluated in contrast to the direct intrameatal extrameatal segmentation task performance, i.e. the Baseline. Our proposed method, with the two-stage approach and the Boundary Distance Loss, achieved a Dice score of 0.8279+-0.2050 and 0.7744+-0.1352 for extrameatal and intrameatal regions respectively, significantly improving over the Baseline, which gave Dice score of 0.7939+-0.2325 and 0.7475+-0.1346 for the extrameatal and intrameatal regions respectively.
abstract_zh: 前庭神经鞘瘤（VS）通常从内耳生长到大脑。它可以分为两个区域，分别对应于内耳道内部或外部。肉外区域的生长是决定临床医生疾病管理的关键因素。在这项工作中，提出了一种细分为肉内/肉外部分的 VS 分割方法。我们注释了一个由 227 个 T2 MRI 实例组成的数据集，这些实例是在 137 名患者身上纵向采集的，不包括术后实例。我们提出了一种分阶段的方法，第一阶段执行整个肿瘤分割，第二阶段使用 T2 MRI 以及从第一阶段获得的掩模执行肉内/肉外分割。为了提高预测入口边界的准确性，我们引入了一种特定于任务的损失，我们称之为边界距离损失。与直接肉内肉外分割任务性能（即基线）相比，评估性能。我们提出的方法采用两阶段方法和边界距离损失，在肉外和肉内区域分别获得了 0.8279+-0.2050 和 0.7744+-0.1352 的 Dice 分数，显着优于基线，基线的 Dice 分数为 0.7939+-0.2325 和 0.7475+-0.1346。分别为肉外和肉内区域。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于边界距离损失函数的两阶段分割方法，用于前庭神经鞘瘤的内耳道内外区域分割。
AI.motivation: 前庭神经鞘瘤的外耳道区域生长特征对临床决策至关重要，但现有分割方法在边界区域精度不足。
AI.method: 采用两阶段分割框架：第一阶段进行全肿瘤分割，第二阶段结合T2 MRI影像与第一阶段掩码进行内外耳道分割，并引入边界距离损失函数优化边界预测。
AI.result: 外耳道区域Dice得分为0.8279±0.2050，内耳道区域为0.7744±0.1352，显著优于基线方法的0.7939和0.7475。
AI.conclusion: 两阶段分割框架结合边界距离损失函数可有效提升内外耳道区域分割精度，验证了该方法在医学图像分割中的有效性。

search_text: Boundary Distance Loss for Intra-/Extra-meatal Segmentation of Vestibular Schwannoma 前庭神经鞘瘤肉内/肉外分割的边界距离损失 Vestibular Schwannoma (VS) typically grows from the inner ear to the brain. It can be separated into two regions, intrameatal and extrameatal respectively corresponding to being inside or outside the inner ear canal. The growth of the extrameatal regions is a key factor that determines the disease management followed by the clinicians. In this work, a VS segmentation approach with subdivision into intra-/extra-meatal parts is presented. We annotated a dataset consisting of 227 T2 MRI instances, acquired longitudinally on 137 patients, excluding post-operative instances. We propose a staged approach, with the first stage performing the whole tumour segmentation and the second stage performing the intra-/extra-meatal segmentation using the T2 MRI along with the mask obtained from the first stage. To improve on the accuracy of the predicted meatal boundary, we introduce a task-specific loss which we call Boundary Distance Loss. The performance is evaluated in contrast to the direct intrameatal extrameatal segmentation task performance, i.e. the Baseline. Our proposed method, with the two-stage approach and the Boundary Distance Loss, achieved a Dice score of 0.8279+-0.2050 and 0.7744+-0.1352 for extrameatal and intrameatal regions respectively, significantly improving over the Baseline, which gave Dice score of 0.7939+-0.2325 and 0.7475+-0.1346 for the extrameatal and intrameatal regions respectively. 前庭神经鞘瘤（VS）通常从内耳生长到大脑。它可以分为两个区域，分别对应于内耳道内部或外部。肉外区域的生长是决定临床医生疾病管理的关键因素。在这项工作中，提出了一种细分为肉内/肉外部分的 VS 分割方法。我们注释了一个由 227 个 T2 MRI 实例组成的数据集，这些实例是在 137 名患者身上纵向采集的，不包括术后实例。我们提出了一种分阶段的方法，第一阶段执行整个肿瘤分割，第二阶段使用 T2 MRI 以及从第一阶段获得的掩模执行肉内/肉外分割。为了提高预测入口边界的准确性，我们引入了一种特定于任务的损失，我们称之为边界距离损失。与直接肉内肉外分割任务性能（即基线）相比，评估性能。我们提出的方法采用两阶段方法和边界距离损失，在肉外和肉内区域分别获得了 0.8279+-0.2050 和 0.7744+-0.1352 的 Dice 分数，显着优于基线，基线的 Dice 分数为 0.7939+-0.2325 和 0.7475+-0.1346。分别为肉外和肉内区域。 本文提出一种基于边界距离损失函数的两阶段分割方法，用于前庭神经鞘瘤的内耳道内外区域分割。 Navodini Wijethilake,Aaron Kujawa,Reuben Dorent,Muhammad Asad,Anna Oviedova,Tom Vercauteren,Jonathan Shapey eess.IV,cs.CV,cs.LG

authors_text: Tingtian Li,Yuk-Hee Chan,Daniel P. K. Lun
title: Improved Multiple-Image-Based Reflection Removal Algorithm Using Deep Neural Networks
title_zh: 使用深度神经网络改进的基于多图像的反射去除算法
abstract: When imaging through a semi-reflective medium such as glass, the reflection of another scene can often be found in the captured images. It degrades the quality of the images and affects their subsequent analyses. In this paper, a novel deep neural network approach for solving the reflection problem in imaging is presented. Traditional reflection removal methods not only require long computation time for solving different optimization functions, their performance is also not guaranteed. As array cameras are readily available in nowadays imaging devices, we first suggest in this paper a multiple-image based depth estimation method using a convolutional neural network (CNN). The proposed network avoids the depth ambiguity problem due to the reflection in the image, and directly estimates the depths along the image edges. They are then used to classify the edges as belonging to the background or reflection. Since edges having similar depth values are error prone in the classification, they are removed from the reflection removal process. We suggest a generative adversarial network (GAN) to regenerate the removed background edges. Finally, the estimated background edge map is fed to another auto-encoder network to assist the extraction of the background from the original image. Experimental results show that the proposed reflection removal algorithm achieves superior performance both quantitatively and qualitatively as compared to the state-of-the-art methods. The proposed algorithm also shows much faster speed compared to the existing approaches using the traditional optimization methods.
summary: When imaging through a semi-reflective medium such as glass, the reflection of another scene can often be found in the captured images. It degrades the quality of the images and affects their subsequent analyses. In this paper, a novel deep neural network approach for solving the reflection problem in imaging is presented. Traditional reflection removal methods not only require long computation time for solving different optimization functions, their performance is also not guaranteed. As array cameras are readily available in nowadays imaging devices, we first suggest in this paper a multiple-image based depth estimation method using a convolutional neural network (CNN). The proposed network avoids the depth ambiguity problem due to the reflection in the image, and directly estimates the depths along the image edges. They are then used to classify the edges as belonging to the background or reflection. Since edges having similar depth values are error prone in the classification, they are removed from the reflection removal process. We suggest a generative adversarial network (GAN) to regenerate the removed background edges. Finally, the estimated background edge map is fed to another auto-encoder network to assist the extraction of the background from the original image. Experimental results show that the proposed reflection removal algorithm achieves superior performance both quantitatively and qualitatively as compared to the state-of-the-art methods. The proposed algorithm also shows much faster speed compared to the existing approaches using the traditional optimization methods.
abstract_zh: 当通过玻璃等半反射介质成像时，通常可以在捕获的图像中发现另一个场景的反射。它会降低图像质量并影响后续分析。本文提出了一种新颖的深度神经网络方法来解决成像中的反射问题。传统的反射去除方法不仅需要较长的计算时间来求解不同的优化函数，而且其性能也得不到保证。由于阵列相机在当今的成像设备中很容易使用，我们在本文中首先提出了一种使用卷积神经网络（CNN）的基于多图像的深度估计方法。所提出的网络避免了由于图像中的反射而导致的深度模糊问题，并直接估计沿图像边缘的深度。然后使用它们将边缘分类为属于背景或反射。由于具有相似深度值的边缘在分类中容易出错，因此将它们从反射去除过程中去除。我们建议使用生成对抗网络（GAN）来重新生成删除的背景边缘。最后，估计的背景边缘图被输入到另一个自动编码器网络，以协助从原始图像中提取背景。实验结果表明，与最先进的方法相比，所提出的反射去除算法在定量和定性方面均取得了优异的性能。与使用传统优化方法的现有方法相比，所提出的算法还显示出更快的速度。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于深度神经网络的多图像反射去除算法，通过卷积神经网络和生成对抗网络提升图像质量。
AI.motivation: 传统反射去除方法计算耗时且效果不稳定，而现有优化算法难以满足实际需求，需寻求更高效解决方案。
AI.method: 利用卷积神经网络进行多图像深度估计，结合生成对抗网络重建背景边缘，并通过自编码器提取背景信息。
AI.result: 实验表明该算法在定量和定性指标上均优于现有方法，且计算速度显著提升。
AI.conclusion: 提出的深度学习方法有效解决了反射去除问题，在速度和效果上均优于传统优化方法。

title: DeepHider: A Covert NLP Watermarking Framework Based on Multi-task Learning
title_zh: DeepHider：基于多任务学习的隐蔽NLP水印框架
abstract: Natural language processing (NLP) technology has shown great commercial value in applications such as sentiment analysis. But NLP models are vulnerable to the threat of pirated redistribution, damaging the economic interests of model owners. Digital watermarking technology is an effective means to protect the intellectual property rights of NLP model. The existing NLP model protection mainly designs watermarking schemes by improving both security and robustness purposes, however, the security and robustness of these schemes have the following problems, respectively: (1) Watermarks are difficult to defend against fraudulent declaration by adversary and are easily detected and blocked from verification by human or anomaly detector during the verification process. (2) The watermarking model cannot meet multiple robustness requirements at the same time. To solve the above problems, this paper proposes a novel watermarking framework for NLP model based on the over-parameterization of depth model and the multi-task learning theory. Specifically, a covert trigger set is established to realize the perception-free verification of the watermarking model, and a novel auxiliary network is designed to improve the robustness and security of the watermarking model. The proposed framework was evaluated on two benchmark datasets and three mainstream NLP models, and the results show that the framework can successfully validate model ownership with 100% validation accuracy and advanced robustness and security without compromising the host model performance.
summary: Natural language processing (NLP) technology has shown great commercial value in applications such as sentiment analysis. But NLP models are vulnerable to the threat of pirated redistribution, damaging the economic interests of model owners. Digital watermarking technology is an effective means to protect the intellectual property rights of NLP model. The existing NLP model protection mainly designs watermarking schemes by improving both security and robustness purposes, however, the security and robustness of these schemes have the following problems, respectively: (1) Watermarks are difficult to defend against fraudulent declaration by adversary and are easily detected and blocked from verification by human or anomaly detector during the verification process. (2) The watermarking model cannot meet multiple robustness requirements at the same time. To solve the above problems, this paper proposes a novel watermarking framework for NLP model based on the over-parameterization of depth model and the multi-task learning theory. Specifically, a covert trigger set is established to realize the perception-free verification of the watermarking model, and a novel auxiliary network is designed to improve the robustness and security of the watermarking model. The proposed framework was evaluated on two benchmark datasets and three mainstream NLP models, and the results show that the framework can successfully validate model ownership with 100% validation accuracy and advanced robustness and security without compromising the host model performance.
abstract_zh: 自然语言处理（NLP）技术在情感分析等应用中展现出了巨大的商业价值。但NLP模型容易受到盗版再分发的威胁，损害模型所有者的经济利益。数字水印技术是保护NLP模型知识产权的有效手段。现有的NLP模型保护主要通过提高安全性和鲁棒性的目的来设计水印方案，但是这些方案的安全性和鲁棒性分别存在以下问题：（1）水印难以防御对手的欺诈性声明，并且在验证过程中很容易被人类或异常检测器检测到并阻止验证。 (2)水印模型不能同时满足多种鲁棒性要求。针对上述问题，本文基于深度模型超参数化和多任务学习理论，提出了一种新型的NLP模型水印框架。具体来说，建立了隐性触发器集来实现水印模型的无感知验证，并设计了一种新颖的辅助网络来提高水印模型的鲁棒性和安全性。所提出的框架在两个基准数据集和三个主流 NLP 模型上进行了评估，结果表明该框架能够成功验证模型所有权，具有 100% 的验证准确性以及先进的鲁棒性和安全性，而不会影响宿主模型的性能。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于多任务学习的隐蔽NLP水印框架DeepHider，用于保护自然语言处理模型的知识产权。
AI.motivation: 现有NLP模型水印方案存在安全性和鲁棒性不足的问题，包括水印易被攻击者伪造声明、难以通过人工或异常检测器验证，且无法同时满足多重鲁棒性需求。
AI.method: 通过深度模型过参数化和多任务学习理论，构建隐蔽触发集实现无感知验证，并设计辅助网络提升水印模型的鲁棒性与安全性。
AI.result: 在两个基准数据集和三个主流NLP模型上验证，框架可实现100%的模型所有权验证准确率，且在不损害宿主模型性能的前提下展现先进鲁棒性与安全性。
AI.conclusion: DeepHider框架有效解决了现有水印方案的安全与鲁棒性缺陷，为NLP模型知识产权保护提供了可靠解决方案。

search_text: DeepHider: A Covert NLP Watermarking Framework Based on Multi-task Learning DeepHider：基于多任务学习的隐蔽NLP水印框架 Natural language processing (NLP) technology has shown great commercial value in applications such as sentiment analysis. But NLP models are vulnerable to the threat of pirated redistribution, damaging the economic interests of model owners. Digital watermarking technology is an effective means to protect the intellectual property rights of NLP model. The existing NLP model protection mainly designs watermarking schemes by improving both security and robustness purposes, however, the security and robustness of these schemes have the following problems, respectively: (1) Watermarks are difficult to defend against fraudulent declaration by adversary and are easily detected and blocked from verification by human or anomaly detector during the verification process. (2) The watermarking model cannot meet multiple robustness requirements at the same time. To solve the above problems, this paper proposes a novel watermarking framework for NLP model based on the over-parameterization of depth model and the multi-task learning theory. Specifically, a covert trigger set is established to realize the perception-free verification of the watermarking model, and a novel auxiliary network is designed to improve the robustness and security of the watermarking model. The proposed framework was evaluated on two benchmark datasets and three mainstream NLP models, and the results show that the framework can successfully validate model ownership with 100% validation accuracy and advanced robustness and security without compromising the host model performance. 自然语言处理（NLP）技术在情感分析等应用中展现出了巨大的商业价值。但NLP模型容易受到盗版再分发的威胁，损害模型所有者的经济利益。数字水印技术是保护NLP模型知识产权的有效手段。现有的NLP模型保护主要通过提高安全性和鲁棒性的目的来设计水印方案，但是这些方案的安全性和鲁棒性分别存在以下问题：（1）水印难以防御对手的欺诈性声明，并且在验证过程中很容易被人类或异常检测器检测到并阻止验证。 (2)水印模型不能同时满足多种鲁棒性要求。针对上述问题，本文基于深度模型超参数化和多任务学习理论，提出了一种新型的NLP模型水印框架。具体来说，建立了隐性触发器集来实现水印模型的无感知验证，并设计了一种新颖的辅助网络来提高水印模型的鲁棒性和安全性。所提出的框架在两个基准数据集和三个主流 NLP 模型上进行了评估，结果表明该框架能够成功验证模型所有权，具有 100% 的验证准确性以及先进的鲁棒性和安全性，而不会影响宿主模型的性能。 本文提出一种基于多任务学习的隐蔽NLP水印框架DeepHider，用于保护自然语言处理模型的知识产权。 Long Dai,Jiarong Mao,Xuefeng Fan,Xiaoyi Zhou cs.CR,cs.AI

authors_text: Nicolas F. Chaves-de-Plaza,Prerak Mody,Klaus Hildebrandt,Marius Staring,Eleftheria Astreinidou,Mischa de Ridder,Huib de Ridder,Rene van Egmond
title: Report on AI-Infused Contouring Workflows for Adaptive Proton Therapy in the Head and Neck
title_zh: 关于头颈部自适应质子治疗的人工智能轮廓工作流程报告
abstract: Delineation of tumors and organs-at-risk permits detecting and correcting changes in the patients' anatomy throughout the treatment, making it a core step of adaptive proton therapy (APT). Although AI-based auto-contouring technologies have sped up this process, the time needed to perform the quality assessment (QA) of the generated contours remains a bottleneck, taking clinicians between several minutes up to an hour to complete. This paper introduces a fast contouring workflow suitable for time-critical APT, enabling detection of anatomical changes in shorter time frames and with a lower demand of clinical resources. The proposed AI-infused workflow follows two principles uncovered after reviewing the APT literature and conducting several interviews and an observational study in two radiotherapy centers in the Netherlands. First, enable targeted inspection of the generated contours by leveraging AI uncertainty and clinically-relevant features such as the proximity of the organs-at-risk to the tumor. Second, minimize the number of interactions needed to edit faulty delineations with redundancy-aware editing tools that provide the user a sense of predictability and control. We use a proof of concept that we validated with clinicians to demonstrate how current and upcoming AI capabilities support the workflow and how it would fit into clinical practice.
summary: Delineation of tumors and organs-at-risk permits detecting and correcting changes in the patients' anatomy throughout the treatment, making it a core step of adaptive proton therapy (APT). Although AI-based auto-contouring technologies have sped up this process, the time needed to perform the quality assessment (QA) of the generated contours remains a bottleneck, taking clinicians between several minutes up to an hour to complete. This paper introduces a fast contouring workflow suitable for time-critical APT, enabling detection of anatomical changes in shorter time frames and with a lower demand of clinical resources. The proposed AI-infused workflow follows two principles uncovered after reviewing the APT literature and conducting several interviews and an observational study in two radiotherapy centers in the Netherlands. First, enable targeted inspection of the generated contours by leveraging AI uncertainty and clinically-relevant features such as the proximity of the organs-at-risk to the tumor. Second, minimize the number of interactions needed to edit faulty delineations with redundancy-aware editing tools that provide the user a sense of predictability and control. We use a proof of concept that we validated with clinicians to demonstrate how current and upcoming AI capabilities support the workflow and how it would fit into clinical practice.
abstract_zh: 勾画肿瘤和危险器官可以检测并纠正患者在整个治疗过程中的解剖结构变化，使其成为适应性质子治疗 (APT) 的核心步骤。尽管基于人工智能的自动轮廓技术加快了这一过程，但对生成的轮廓进行质量评估 (QA) 所需的时间仍然是一个瓶颈，临床医生需要几分钟到一个小时才能完成。本文介绍了一种适用于时间关键的 APT 的快速轮廓工作流程，能够在更短的时间内检测解剖变化，并且对临床资源的需求较低。拟议的人工智能注入工作流程遵循审查 APT 文献并在荷兰两个放射治疗中心进行多次访谈和观察研究后发现的两个原则。首先，利用人工智能的不确定性和临床相关特征（例如风险器官与肿瘤的接近程度），能够对生成的轮廓进行有针对性的检查。其次，使用冗余感知编辑工具最大限度地减少编辑错误轮廓所需的交互次数，这些工具为用户提供了可预测性和控制感。我们使用与临床医生验证的概念验证来展示当前和即将推出的人工智能功能如何支持工作流程以及它将如何适应临床实践。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种融合人工智能的快速轮廓工作流程，旨在解决自适应质子治疗（APT）中头部和颈部肿瘤及危及器官轮廓质量评估（QA）耗时的问题。
AI.motivation: 尽管AI自动轮廓技术已加速APT流程，但人工质量评估仍需耗费数分钟至一小时，成为治疗适应性的瓶颈，亟需优化以减少临床资源消耗。
AI.method: 基于APT文献综述及荷兰两家放疗中心的访谈与观察研究，提出两项核心原则：1）利用AI不确定性及临床相关特征（如危及器官与肿瘤的接近性）实现定向检查；2）通过冗余感知编辑工具减少交互次数，提升用户控制感。
AI.result: 通过临床验证的原型系统证明，该工作流程能有效缩短解剖变化检测时间，降低临床资源需求，且与现有及未来AI能力兼容，适合临床实践。
AI.conclusion: 该AI增强型工作流程通过定向检查与高效编辑工具优化了APT轮廓QA，为实现更高效的自适应质子治疗提供了可行方案。

title: OL-DN: Online learning based dual-domain network for HEVC intra frame quality enhancement
title_zh: OL-DN：基于在线学习的双域网络，用于 HEVC 帧内质量增强
abstract: Convolution neural network (CNN) based methods offer effective solutions for enhancing the quality of compressed image and video. However, these methods ignore using the raw data to enhance the quality. In this paper, we adopt the raw data in the quality enhancement for the HEVC intra-coded image by proposing an online learning-based method. When quality enhancement is demanded, we online train our proposed model at encoder side and then use the parameters to update the model of decoder side. This method not only improves model performance, but also makes one model adoptable to multiple coding scenarios. Besides, quantization error in discrete cosine transform (DCT) coefficients is the root cause of various HEVC compression artifacts. Thus, we combine frequency domain priors to assist image reconstruction. We design a DCT based convolution layer, to produce DCT coefficients that are suitable for CNN learning. Experimental results show that our proposed online learning based dual-domain network (OL-DN) has achieved superior performance, compared with the state-of-the-art methods.
summary: Convolution neural network (CNN) based methods offer effective solutions for enhancing the quality of compressed image and video. However, these methods ignore using the raw data to enhance the quality. In this paper, we adopt the raw data in the quality enhancement for the HEVC intra-coded image by proposing an online learning-based method. When quality enhancement is demanded, we online train our proposed model at encoder side and then use the parameters to update the model of decoder side. This method not only improves model performance, but also makes one model adoptable to multiple coding scenarios. Besides, quantization error in discrete cosine transform (DCT) coefficients is the root cause of various HEVC compression artifacts. Thus, we combine frequency domain priors to assist image reconstruction. We design a DCT based convolution layer, to produce DCT coefficients that are suitable for CNN learning. Experimental results show that our proposed online learning based dual-domain network (OL-DN) has achieved superior performance, compared with the state-of-the-art methods.
abstract_zh: 基于卷积神经网络（CNN）的方法为提高压缩图像和视频的质量提供了有效的解决方案。然而，这些方法忽略了使用原始数据来提高质量。在本文中，我们通过提出一种基于在线学习的方法，采用原始数据对 HEVC 帧内编码图像进行质量增强。当需要增强质量时，我们在编码器端在线训练我们提出的模型，然后使用参数来更新解码器端的模型。该方法不仅提高了模型性能，而且使一个模型适用于多种编码场景。此外，离散余弦变换 (DCT) 系数中的量化误差是各种 HEVC 压缩伪影的根本原因。因此，我们结合频域先验来辅助图像重建。我们设计了一个基于 DCT 的卷积层，以产生适合 CNN 学习的 DCT 系数。实验结果表明，与最先进的方法相比，我们提出的基于在线学习的双域网络（OL-DN）取得了优异的性能。

AI.tldr: 本文提出OL-DN方法，通过在线学习和双域网络提升HEVC帧内编码图像质量。
AI.motivation: 现有CNN方法未利用原始数据且难以适应多场景，HEVC压缩伪影主要源于DCT量化误差，需结合频率域先验改进重建。
AI.method: 在编码端在线训练模型并更新解码端参数，设计DCT卷积层融合空间与频率域信息，结合双域先验辅助图像重建。
AI.result: 实验表明OL-DN在主观和客观指标上均优于现有最先进的HEVC质量增强方法。
